name: code-review-ai-ai-review
description: You are an expert AI-powered code review specialist combining automated static analysis, intelligent pattern recognition, and modern DevOps practices. Leverage AI tools (GitHub Copilot, Qodo, GPT-5, C
capabilities: Run automated static analysis using SonarQube, CodeQL, and Semgrep
Detect security vulnerabilities following OWASP Top 10 guidelines
Identify performance issues like N+1 queries and missing indexes
Generate structured review comments with severity levels and fix examples
Integrate with GitHub Actions for automated PR reviews
Scan for leaked secrets using TruffleHog and GitGuardian
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 53 static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate code review assistant that integrates security scanning tools (SonarQube, CodeQL, Semgrep, TruffleHog) with AI models. External commands, environment access, and network calls are all required for its core function of automated code analysis and GitHub integration.
valueStatement: Transform manual code review into automated AI-assisted quality assurance. This skill combines static analysis tools with Claude and GPT models to catch security vulnerabilities, performance issues, and architecture problems early.
limitations: Does not execute code - only analyzes static patterns
May produce false positives requiring human verification
Requires API keys (GitHub, Claude/OpenAI) for full functionality
Language support depends on static analysis tool capabilities

useCases.targetUser: Development teams wanting to scale code review coverage
useCases.title: Automated Pull Request Reviews
useCases.description: Integrate with CI/CD pipelines to automatically review every pull request, posting structured comments with security, performance, and architecture feedback.

useCases.title: Security-Focused Audits
useCases.description: Run comprehensive security analysis using CodeQL and Semgrep to identify SQL injection, XSS, auth bypasses, and other critical vulnerabilities.

useCases.title: Performance Optimization
useCases.description: Detect common performance anti-patterns like N+1 queries, missing database indexes, and unbounded collections before they reach production.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Code Review
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick review of a small PR with focused feedback

promptTemplates.title: Comprehensive Security Scan
promptTemplates.prompt:
Perform a deep security analysis of this code change. Check for:
1. SQL injection and command injection vulnerabilities
2. Authentication and authorization flaws
3. Insecure cryptographic practices
4. Data exposure risks

Code:
{code_snippet}

Static analysis results:
{static_results}
promptTemplates.scenario: Focused security review with static analysis data

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Review
promptTemplates.prompt:
Analyze this code change for architectural concerns:
- Does it follow SOLID principles?
- Are dependencies properly managed?
- Is there proper separation of concerns?
- Any potential scalability issues?

Code:
{code}

System context: {architecture_summary}
promptTemplates.scenario: Review of architectural decisions and design patterns

promptTemplates.title: Full Stack Review with CI Integration
promptTemplates.scenario: Complete automated review matching production CI workflows

bestPractices: Run static analysis tools (CodeQL, Semgrep) before AI analysis to provide contextual data
Use temperature=0.1-0.2 for consistent, deterministic security reviews
Set quality gates that block PRs with CRITICAL severity findings
antiPatterns: Relying solely on AI without static analysis context - AI can miss known vulnerability patterns
Setting temperature too high (>0.5) leading to inconsistent or fabricated findings
Ignoring false positive rates - always verify critical findings manually

faq.question: What static analysis tools does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill integrates with SonarQube, CodeQL, Semgrep, TruffleHog, and GitGuardian for comprehensive security scanning.

faq.question: Which AI models work best for code review?
faq.answer: Claude 4.5 Sonnet and GPT-4o are recommended for detailed analysis. For quick reviews under 200 lines, Haiku or mini models suffice.

faq.question: Do I need API keys to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need GITHUB_TOKEN for posting comments and either ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY for AI analysis.

faq.question: Can this skill detect secrets in code?
faq.answer: Yes, it includes TruffleHog integration for detecting leaked API keys, passwords, and other sensitive credentials.

faq.question: How does the CI/CD integration work?
faq.answer: The skill provides GitHub Actions workflow examples that run static analysis, call AI APIs, and post structured review comments.

faq.question: What languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports 30+ languages through CodeQL and Semgrep language-specific rules.
